locations.name: National Museum of Natural History
locations.description: Features an iconic "Tree of Life" atrium housing significant zoological, botanical, and geological specimens.

locations.name: National Museum of Anthropology
locations.description: Housed in a neoclassical building, this museum showcases the rich cultural heritage and archaeological history of the Philippines.

locations.name: National Museum of Fine Arts
locations.description: Houses major works by Filipino masters, including Juan Luna's Spoliarium, along with various classical and contemporary art pieces.

locations.name: Digital National Art Museum (DNA Museum)
locations.description: A modern, immersive digital art experience that creatively engages visitors with technology and art.

locations.name: Museo Sugbo
locations.description: Former Cebu provincial jail converted into a museum showcasing Cebu’s history and culture from the pre-colonial period to modern times.

locations.name: Negros Museum
locations.description: Features the history, sugar industry, arts, and lifestyle of Negros Occidental.

locations.name: Ponce Suites Gallery Hotel
locations.description: An art-inspired hotel filled with sculptures and artworks by Kublai Millan.

locations.name: Fort Santiago
locations.description: A significant historical citadel used as a defense fortress and prison, famously associated with national hero José Rizal.

locations.name: Intramuros
locations.description: The Manila's historic "Walled City," a preserved colonial district featuring cobblestone streets, iconic Spanish architecture, and key landmarks like Fort Santiago and San Agustin Church.

locations.name: Rizal Park
locations.description: Known as Luneta, this historic urban park is the site of the Rizal Monument and a central gathering place for national commemoration.

locations.name: Baluarte de San Diego
locations.description: A historic stone fortification built in the 16th century, now preserved as a scenic garden site.

locations.name: Corregidor Island
locations.description: A strategic island fortress in Manila Bay, critical during WWII; features ruins, tunnels, and memorials.

locations.name: Fort San Pedro
locations.description: The oldest triangular fort in the Philippines built by the Spanish in 1565.

locations.name: Aguinaldo Shrine
locations.description: Historic house where Philippine Independence was declared on June 12, 1898.

locations.name: Barasoain Church
locations.description: Known as the “Cradle of Democracy in the East” where the First Philippine Congress was held.

locations.name: Baler Public Market
locations.description: The central hub for locals, offering fresh produce, local delicacies like suman, and regional souvenirs.

locations.name: Baguio Night Market
locations.description: A bustling outdoor night market along Harrison Road where you can find secondhand clothing (ukay-ukay), accessories, and local street food.

locations.name: Pampanga Food Hubs
locations.description: Pampanga is the "Culinary Capital of the Philippines." These hubs offer authentic Kapampangan dishes, including world-famous sisig.

locations.name: Salcedo Weekend Market
locations.description: One of Metro Manila’s most popular and well-loved outdoor weekend markets. A vibrant hub of food, fresh produce, shopping, and community every Saturday.

locations.name: Quiapo Market
locations.description: One of the rawest and most chaotic street food hubs. Famous for isaw (grilled intestines), kwek-kwek, palabok, sotanghon, balut, and fresh lumpia.

locations.name: Divisoria Market
locations.description: A massive, bustling bargain paradise with excellent street snacks, fresh goods, and local eats. Great for cheap, authentic Filipino street food while shopping.

locations.name: Carbon Market
locations.description: The oldest and largest public market in Cebu. Known for fresh seafood, danggit (dried fish), puso (hanging rice), and Cebu-style lechon.

locations.name: Taboan Public Market
locations.description: Specializes in dried fish (danggit), dried mangoes, and other Cebuano delicacies. A must for seafood lovers and those seeking authentic provincial flavors.

locations.name: Roxas Night Market
locations.description: A lively night market with affordable street food, fresh seafood, and Mindanao influences. One of the wilder and more energetic spots in the south.

locations.name: Enchanted Kingdom
locations.description: The country’s premier theme park featuring thrill rides like the Space Shuttle and the Agila EKsperience.

locations.name: Star City
locations.description: An indoor/outdoor amusement park located in Pasay City, known for its snow world and diverse carnival rides.

locations.name: Manila Ocean Park
locations.description: A world-class oceanarium and marine-themed park behind the Quirino Grandstand in Manila.

locations.name: SuperPark Philippines
locations.description: A modern indoor activity park with over 20 interactive games, trampolines, climbing walls, slides, and digital experiences like Floor is Lava.

locations.name: Dinosaurs Island Clark
locations.description: First animatronic dinosaur theme park featuring life-sized moving dinosaurs, educational exhibits, and interactive attractions.

locations.name: Sky Ranch Pampanga
locations.description: A popular amusement park featuring thrilling rides like the giant Ferris wheel (Pampanga Eye), roller coasters, and family attractions.

locations.name: Gootopia
locations.description: Philippines’ first slime-themed fun house featuring gooey challenges, slime-making, interactive games, and obstacles.

locations.name: DreamPlay by DreamWorks
locations.description: World’s first DreamWorks-themed indoor interactive play center featuring 12 attractions with characters from Shrek, Trolls, Kung Fu Panda, and more.

locations.name: Anjo World Theme Park
locations.description: Cebu’s first and largest world-class amusement park featuring over 12 rides, family attractions, and an indoor Snow World.

locations.name: Space & Time Cube
locations.description: Philippines’ first immersive digital art museum featuring 18-20 themed attractions with interactive installations, optical illusions, LED tunnels, and somatosensory games.

locations.name: Dessert Museum
locations.description: A fun, interactive museum with 8+ colorful dessert-themed rooms for photos, free desserts, and unlimited ice cream.

locations.name: SM by the Bay Amusement Park
locations.description: A seaside outdoor amusement park beside SM Mall of Asia featuring thrilling rides, family attractions, and scenic Manila Bay views.

locations.name: Mayon Volcano View Deck
locations.description: Various spots in Albay offering panoramic views of the world-famous "perfect cone" volcano.

locations.name: Banaue Rice Terraces
locations.description: Ancient man-made rice paddies carved into the mountains, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

locations.name: Cape Bojeador Lighthouse
locations.description: A historic Spanish-colonial lighthouse in Burgos, Ilocos Norte, offering stunning views of the West Philippine Sea.

locations.name: Chocolate Hills
locations.description: Consists of more than 1,200 naturally formed hills that turn brown during dry season.

locations.name: Vigan Calle Crisologo
locations.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site known for preserved Spanish colonial streets and ancestral houses.

locations.name: Burnham Park
locations.description: A famous urban park in the Summer Capital of the Philippines known for boating, biking, and cool weather.

locations.name: El Nido Lagoons
locations.description: Iconic turquoise lagoons surrounded by limestone karst cliffs, accessible via island-hopping tours (Tour A).

locations.name: Hundred Islands
locations.description: A national park in Alaminos, Pangasinan, consisting of 124 islands scattered in the Lingayen Gulf.

locations.name: Subic Beaches
locations.description: Subic’s beaches—such as All Hands Beach, Camayan, and Hidden Beach—offer family-friendly coastal spots within a freeport zone.

locations.name: Boracay Island
locations.description: Known worldwide for its white sand beaches, nightlife, and water activities.

locations.name: Kawasan Falls
locations.description: Popular for canyoneering, cliff jumping, and turquoise blue waterfalls.

locations.name: Siargao Island
locations.description: Known as the surfing capital of the Philippines and famous for lagoons, beaches, and island hopping.

locations.name: Apo Island
locations.description: Famous for snorkeling with sea turtles and protected coral reefs.

locations.name: Mount Pulag
locations.description: As the highest mountain in Luzon, it is famous for its breathtaking sea of clouds, unique grassland ecosystem, and pine forests.

locations.name: Taal Volcano
locations.description: While the volcano remains an active geological site, boat tours from Talisay, Batangas, allow visitors to cruise the lake and view the crater's aftermath.

locations.name: Sagada Caves
locations.description: Known as the "Big Cave," it is the most popular spelunking site in Sagada. It features massive rock formations, underground pools, and challenging terrain.

locations.name: Puerto Princesa Underground River
locations.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New 7 Wonders of Nature.

locations.name: Lake Sebu
locations.description: Known for T’boli culture, Seven Falls, and scenic lakes.

locations.name: Mount Apo
locations.description: Mount Apo Natural Park is the highest mountain in the Philippines. It is famous for challenging hikes, mossy forests, and panoramic summit views.

tripRoutes.name: National Museum of Natural History
tripRoutes.route: Accessible via taxi, jeepney, or the United Nations LRT-1 station.

tripRoutes.name: National Museum of Anthropology
tripRoutes.route: Accessible via taxi, jeepney, or the United Nations LRT-1 station.

tripRoutes.name: National Museum of Fine Arts
tripRoutes.route: Walkable from the National Museum of Anthropology and Natural History.

tripRoutes.name: Digital National Art Museum (DNA Museum)
tripRoutes.route: Take a taxi or jeepney from Taft Avenue toward the Luneta/Ocean Park area.

tripRoutes.name: Museo Sugbo
tripRoutes.route: From Mactan-Cebu Airport, ride a taxi or MyBus to Cebu City proper, then ride a jeepney to Museo Sugbo.

tripRoutes.name: Negros Museum
tripRoutes.route: From Bacolod-Silay Airport, ride a shuttle or taxi to Bacolod City proper, then proceed to the Provincial Capitol area.

tripRoutes.name: Ponce Suites Gallery Hotel
tripRoutes.route: From Davao International Airport, ride a taxi to Bajada, Davao City.

tripRoutes.name: Fort Santiago
tripRoutes.route: Accessible via jeepney, taxi, or walking from the Central Terminal LRT station.

tripRoutes.name: Intramuros
tripRoutes.route: Accessible by Intramuros shuttle, taxi, or walking from the Central Terminal LRT station.

tripRoutes.name: Rizal Park
tripRoutes.route: Short walk from the United Nations LRT-1 station or any jeepney heading to Luneta.

tripRoutes.name: Baluarte de San Diego
tripRoutes.route: Located within the southern part of Intramuros. Accessible by pedicab or walking.

tripRoutes.name: Corregidor Island
tripRoutes.route: Book a ferry tour package. Ferries operate from terminals near the CCP complex or Mall of Asia.

tripRoutes.name: Fort San Pedro
tripRoutes.route: From Mactan-Cebu Airport, take a taxi or MyBus to Cebu City and proceed to Plaza Independencia.

tripRoutes.name: Aguinaldo Shrine
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus bound for Cavite/Kawit from the PITX terminal, then ride a tricycle to the shrine.

tripRoutes.name: Barasoain Church
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus bound for Malolos, Bulacan from Monumento or Cubao, then ride a tricycle to the church.

tripRoutes.name: Baler Public Market
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus (JoyBus or Genesis) directly to the Baler terminal, then take a short tricycle ride.

tripRoutes.name: Baguio Night Market
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus to Baguio City. The market is centrally located near Burnham Park along Harrison Road.

tripRoutes.name: Pampanga Food Hubs
tripRoutes.route: Accessible via NLEX. Take a bus to San Fernando or Angeles City.

tripRoutes.name: Salcedo Weekend Market
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Ayala Station, then walk or ride a jeepney to Jaime Velasquez Park in Salcedo Village.

tripRoutes.name: Quiapo Market
tripRoutes.route: Take the LRT-1 to Carriedo Station, then walk to Quiapo Church and the surrounding market.

tripRoutes.name: Divisoria Market
tripRoutes.route: Take the LRT-2 to Recto Station. From Recto, walk or take a short tricycle/jeepney ride to Divisoria.

tripRoutes.name: Carbon Market
tripRoutes.route: From Mactan-Cebu International Airport, take a taxi or airport bus + jeepney to downtown Carbon.

tripRoutes.name: Taboan Public Market
tripRoutes.route: From Mactan-Cebu International Airport, take a taxi or jeepney to Taboan.

tripRoutes.name: Roxas Night Market
tripRoutes.route: From Davao International Airport, take a taxi to Roxas Avenue.

tripRoutes.name: Enchanted Kingdom
tripRoutes.route: Take a shuttle from Makati or a bus from Buendia/Pasay to Balibago, Sta. Rosa.

tripRoutes.name: Star City
tripRoutes.route: Accessible via taxi or jeepney from Taft Avenue/Roxas Boulevard.

tripRoutes.name: Manila Ocean Park
tripRoutes.route: Take the LRT-1 to United Nations Station, then take a short taxi or pedicab ride behind Quirino Grandstand.

tripRoutes.name: SuperPark Philippines
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Ortigas or Ayala, then ride a jeepney/taxi to McKinley Hill, Taguig.

tripRoutes.name: Dinosaurs Island Clark
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus to Dau Terminal or SM Clark, then take a local jeepney/taxi into the Clark Freeport Zone.

tripRoutes.name: Sky Ranch Pampanga
tripRoutes.route: Take a provincial bus to San Fernando / Dau Terminal, then take a jeepney/taxi to SM City Pampanga.

tripRoutes.name: Gootopia
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Taft Avenue Station, then transfer to a jeepney or taxi heading to SM Mall of Asia.

tripRoutes.name: DreamPlay by DreamWorks
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Taft Avenue Station, then ride a bus or taxi directly to City of Dreams Manila.

tripRoutes.name: Anjo World Theme Park
tripRoutes.route: From Mactan-Cebu International Airport, take a taxi or Grab south to Minglanilla.

tripRoutes.name: Space & Time Cube
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Taft Avenue, then ride a jeepney to SM Mall of Asia / S Maison.

tripRoutes.name: Dessert Museum
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Taft Avenue, then ride a jeepney to SM Mall of Asia / S Maison.

tripRoutes.name: SM by the Bay Amusement Park
tripRoutes.route: Take the MRT-3 to Taft Avenue, then ride a jeepney to SM Mall of Asia and walk to the bay area.

tripRoutes.name: Mayon Volcano View Deck
tripRoutes.route: Fly or take a bus to Legazpi, then use local transport or a rental car to visit specific view decks.

tripRoutes.name: Banaue Rice Terraces
tripRoutes.route: Take a direct overnight bus from Manila to Banaue.

tripRoutes.name: Cape Bojeador Lighthouse
tripRoutes.route: Fly or take a bus to Laoag, then hire a car or tricycle for the 45-minute drive to Burgos.

tripRoutes.name: Chocolate Hills
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Bohol (Panglao Airport), then book a van or countryside tour heading to Carmen.

tripRoutes.name: Vigan Calle Crisologo
tripRoutes.route: Take a direct bus from Manila (Cubao/Pasay) to Vigan.

tripRoutes.name: Burnham Park
tripRoutes.route: Take a direct bus from Manila to Baguio City, then take a taxi or jeepney to the park.

tripRoutes.name: El Nido Lagoons
tripRoutes.route: Fly directly to El Nido (Lio Airport), or fly to Puerto Princesa and take a 5-6 hour van transfer to El Nido town.

tripRoutes.name: Hundred Islands
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus from Manila to Alaminos City, then a tricycle to Lucap Wharf to rent a boat.

tripRoutes.name: Subic Beaches
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus bound for Olongapo/Subic via NLEX/SCTEX. From the terminal, take a taxi or rent a car into the Freeport Zone.

tripRoutes.name: Boracay Island
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Caticlan, ride a tricycle to the Jetty Port, take a boat to Boracay Island, and ride a tricycle/van to your station.

tripRoutes.name: Kawasan Falls
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Cebu, take a bus from the South Bus Terminal to Badian, then ride a habal-habal (motorcycle) to the falls.

tripRoutes.name: Siargao Island
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Siargao (Sayak Airport), then ride a van or tricycle to General Luna to join island hopping tours.

tripRoutes.name: Apo Island
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Dumaguete, ride a van or tricycle to Malatapay Port (Dauin), then take a motorized boat to Apo Island.

tripRoutes.name: Mount Pulag
tripRoutes.route: Take a bus to Baguio City. From Baguio, hire a private van or take a local jeepney to the DENR Ranger Station in Kabayan.

tripRoutes.name: Taal Volcano
tripRoutes.route: Drive or take a bus south via SLEX/STAR Tollway to Talisay, Batangas, then hire a local boat to view the island.

tripRoutes.name: Sagada Caves
tripRoutes.route: Take a direct overnight bus from Manila to Sagada, or transit via Baguio. Register at the local Tourism Center in town.

tripRoutes.name: Puerto Princesa Underground River
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Puerto Princesa Airport, take a van or bus to Sabang Wharf, then ride a boat to the Underground River site.

tripRoutes.name: Lake Sebu
tripRoutes.route: Fly to General Santos City, take a bus/van to Koronadal, then transfer to a van bound for Lake Sebu.

tripRoutes.name: Mount Apo
tripRoutes.route: Fly to Davao City, then take a bus/van to a jump-off point like Kapatagan or Sta. Cruz.
